before: A hereditary PPGL clue increases metastatic risk to more than {{c1::40}}%.
after: A {{c1::SDHB mutation}} is a high-risk hereditary PPGL clue because it is associated with a higher risk of metastatic disease.
rule_candidate: When a mutation changes risk interpretation, hide the mutation rather than a supporting percentage unless the threshold itself changes management.
notes: Synthetic example based on the weak-cloze failure pattern.

before: When meeting an adrenal incidentaloma, PPGL should first be excluded.
after: When an adrenal incidentaloma is found, it is important to check whether it is PPGL first because unrecognized catecholamine secretion can increase perioperative cardiovascular risk.
rule_candidate: Rewrite source-like phrasing into natural study language when the original wording can imply the wrong priority.
notes: Synthetic wording example; no private source copied.

before: An image appears above the card title, followed immediately by dense explanation text.
after: Place the image below the title, add a one-line cue explaining what the learner should inspect, and separate the caption from the next text block.
rule_candidate: Image cards need layout separation and a cue that states the visual job of the image.
